data.title: United Natural Foods' cyberattack contained but will have 'material' impact on Q4 EPS
data.description:
Shares of United Natural Foods (UNFI were building on Thursday’s late gains after the company assured shareholders the June 5 cybersecurity event has been contained and is now regularly receiving and shipping products to retailers.

The company has also restored the core systems that customers and suppliers use for electronic ordering and invoicing, resulting in “normalized” business operations.

However, since the event resulted in reduced sales volume and increased operational costs, United Natural Foods (NYSE:UNFI) incurred -- and expects to continue to incur -- direct expenses related to the subsequent investigation and remediation of the incident.

“Based on the information currently available to the company, management believes that the incident is reasonably likely to have a material impact on net income/(loss) and adjusted EBITDA for the fourth fiscal quarter of 2025 compared to its internal projections prior to the incident,” United Natural Foods said in a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Cybersecurity insurance is expected to be “adequate” and the full claim and settlement process will extend into FY26.  

Shares are up ~6%.

data.title: Meta's Zuckerberg, execs discuss 'de-investing' in Llama for competitors: report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) executives, including CEO Mark Zuckerberg, have discussed “de-investing” in its Llama family of language models in favor of models made by competitors, The New York Times reported. 

Meta could look to models from OpenAI and Anthropic, the news outlet added, citing two people familiar with the matter. While no final decision has been made on the matter, it would mean turning towards the “closed” approach to models, whereas Llama is open source and can be tweaked by others. 

OpenAI is largely backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY), while Anthropic is backed by Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and others.

“We remain fully committed to developing Llama and plan to have multiple additional releases this year alone,” a Meta spokesperson told Seeking Alpha. OpenAI and Anthropic did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Meta released the first models from its Llama 4 series in April at its LlamaCon event, however the new model did not perform as well as rivals. Additionally, a number of developers left the event feeling underwhelmed, the news outlet added.

Since the release of Llama 4, Meta has gone on a spending spree to catch up with competitors.

Earlier this month, the company announced a deal to spend $14.3B to acquire nearly half of Scale AI. A key aspect of the deal includes Scale CEO Alexandr Wang joining Meta to head its “superintelligence” team focused on artificial general intelligence.

The company has also offered significant pay packages to bring AI researchers from OpenAI into Meta, including highly respected OpenAI researcher Trapit Bansal. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman recently said Meta was offering $100M signing bonuses in an attempt to poach his employees.

Meta is also reportedly in the final stages of acquiring PlayAI, a California-based startup that specializes in generating AI voices that are human-like.

The Zuckerberg-led Meta also reportedly discussed acquiring Perplexity AI prior to its multi-billion dollar investment into Scale AI. Apple (AAPL) has also reportedly discussed acquiring or partnering with Perplexity AI).

Deals with Runway AI and FuriosaAI were also considered, prior to Meta's Scale AI investment.

(This story has been updated with comment from Meta.)

data.title: US supreme court rules in favor of Obamacare task force
data.description:
• The U.S. Supreme Court on Friday ruled in favor of the Trump administration in a case that challenged the constitutionality of the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force, a key element introduced under the Affordable Care Act, also known as Obamacare, in 2010.
  • In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court justices struck down a lower court's ruling that determined that the USPSTF, which plays a key role in determining the type of preventive services health insurers must cover without cost to patients, was composed of invalid appointees.
  • Health insurers: UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Humana (HUM), CVS Health (NYSE:CVS),  Cigna (NYSE:CI), Clover Health (CLOV) and Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), Centene (CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV)

This is a developing story. Check back for more updates.

data.title: Estée Lauder gains after HSBC said the personal care stock has turned a corner
data.description:
The Estée Lauder Companies (NYSE:EL) rallied in late morning trading on Friday after HSBC upgraded the stock to a Buy rating after having it set at Hold. Importantly, the magnitude of the company's restructuring program and recent USD weakness is seen signaling an end to the earnings downgrade cycle.

Analyst Erwan Rambourg said there are clear reasons for investors to be positive on the cosmetics stick. "First, mechanically, like most US-based exporters, the group benefits from a weak USD. Second, and more structurally, the magnitude of the restructuring program, the so-called PRGP (profit recovery and growth plan), is such that margins should now have a solid floor starting this year (FY June 2026) even if sales growth doesn’t rebound sharply," highlighted Rambourg.

Notably, HSBC thinks there is evidence of market share gains for Estée Lauder (NYSE:EL) in key local markets such as the U.S., Japan, and China. On the fundamental side, EL is forecast to double EPS over the next two years, which makes valuation more attractive.

Estée Lauder (EL) has perked up over the last six weeks, with a rise of more than 25% as tariff anxiety from investors has ebbed. The stock still sits well below its 52-week high of $110.19. Estée Lauder (EL) is likely to issue its FQ4 report during the third week of August.

data.title: Enerpac signals cautious stance for Q4 2025 while maintaining $610M–$625M revenue outlook amid tariff and market headwinds
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Enerpac Tool Group Corp. (EPAC) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Paul E. Sternlieb opened the call by highlighting a record third quarter revenue since the relaunch in 2019, noting "we are taking a cautious posture entering the fourth quarter given the increasing level of economic and geopolitical uncertainty." Sternlieb emphasized confidence in Enerpac's ability to outperform industrial peers in a soft sector and discussed the company's innovation strategy and DTA acquisition progress.

  • CFO Darren M. Kozik reported "Enerpac's revenue increased 6% on a reported basis to $159 million in the third quarter of 2025." Kozik detailed organic growth of 2%, with the IT&S business up 1.5% organically and noted 19% growth at Cortland Biomedical. Kozik stated, "Adjusted earnings per share increased 9% to $0.51, driven by higher earnings, a lower effective tax rate and reduced share count."

  • Sternlieb highlighted the new headquarters and innovation lab: "We have invested in our new innovation lab, dedicating a significant portion of the new facility to take our innovation and R&D capabilities to the next level."

Outlook

  • The company maintained its full year fiscal 2025 earnings guidance: "net sales of $610 million to $625 million, representing total revenue growth of 3% to 6% and organic growth of 0% to 2%. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the $150 million to $160 million range. However, based on year-to-date results and current macroeconomic and geopolitical conditions, we anticipate delivering towards the lower half of the range," according to CFO Kozik.

  • Free cash flow guidance remains at $85 million to $95 million for the year.

Financial Results

  • Revenue reached $159 million for Q3 2025, with organic revenue up 2%. The IT&S business saw 1.5% organic growth, and Cortland Biomedical posted 19% growth.

  • Gross profit margin declined 140 basis points year-over-year to 50.4%, mainly due to service project mix and the inclusion of DTA, but was partially offset by higher margins at Cortland Biomedical.

  • Adjusted SG&A improved to 25.5% of sales, down 160 basis points year-over-year. The quarter included a restructuring charge of $5.9 million, with approximately three-quarters related to severance and the remainder a noncash lease impairment.

  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 3.4%, but margin declined 50 basis points year-over-year to 25.9%.

  • Net debt at quarter end was $50 million, with total liquidity of $539 million. Cash flow from operations through the first three quarters was $56 million. Free cash flow increased 24% to $40 million despite $11 million in incremental capital spending.

  • The company repurchased about 330,000 shares for $14 million during the quarter.

Q&A

  • Will Gildea, CJS Securities: Asked about customer reactions to tariffs and macro uncertainty, and if projects were being put on hold. Sternlieb replied, "We've not seen any meaningful movement in terms of project cancellations from our perspective... some may be waiting just to see how things shake out in the current environment and the tariff situation but the underlying needs are there."

  • Gildea asked if revenue was pulled forward in Q3 due to tariffs and about channel inventory. Sternlieb responded, "I wouldn't say anything extremely meaningful. There probably was a little bit of buy-in... but I wouldn't say that there was anything hugely significant."

  • Gildea requested detail on restructuring actions and expected savings. Kozik answered, "This is not a programmatic activity... we just looked at the global uncertainty geopolitical risk and decided it was time to take some of those cost actions... 3/4 of that was severance for people. About 1/4... was a noncash lease impairment."

  • Thomas Lloyd Hayes, CL King: Inquired about pricing actions. Kozik said, "We took one in March and one in May... the real impact will be in the upcoming quarter in the fourth quarter we're in now."

  • Hayes asked about end-market performance, wind business outlook, and M&A appetite. Sternlieb noted ongoing strength in aerospace, infrastructure, and wind in EMEA, stating "we still see opportunities here in the U.S. market... we've not changed our overall strategy." Regarding M&A, Sternlieb said, "we continue to be very active... nothing has changed. Certainly, our focus on remaining highly disciplined, however, that as well has not changed."

  • Robert Samuel Karlov, William Blair: Asked about the net impact of tariffs. Kozik replied, "Our goal for Enerpac is to really remain price cost neutral."

  • Karlov questioned DTA performance relative to guidance. Sternlieb acknowledged, "the business will likely come in a bit shy of our original revenue guidance for the year... revenue has increased on a sequential basis... orders are tracking to more than EUR 20 million."

  • Steven Silver, Argus Research: Focused on the innovation lab's impact. Sternlieb emphasized, "the much more significant benefit will be the time improvement... we're able to dramatically reduce the time that it takes to prototype components and parts, literally from weeks to days or days to hours."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about tariffs, cost actions, and market uncertainty but maintained a neutral tone, asking for specifics on cost structure, pricing actions, and customer sentiment.

  • Management maintained a slightly cautious tone in prepared remarks, shifting to a more defensive stance in Q&A when addressing tariffs and restructuring, with phrases such as "we anticipate delivering towards the lower half of the range" and "This is not a programmatic activity... we just looked at the global uncertainty."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's sentiment shifted from confident to more cautious, especially regarding guidance and macroeconomic headwinds. Analysts’ tone remained probing but not overtly negative.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company reiterated full-year guidance but now expects results toward the lower end of previously stated ranges, reflecting increased caution compared to Q2.

  • Gross margin declined further this quarter, and restructuring actions were more prominent versus the prior period.

  • Management's tone shifted from confident in Q2 to more measured and cautious in Q3, especially regarding economic and geopolitical uncertainties.

  • Analysts continued to focus on tariffs, cost management, restructuring, and DTA integration, mirroring the previous quarter's emphasis but with increased focus on cost actions and pricing.

  • Management highlighted the expanded innovation lab and its expected impact on product development pace, a new development since the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted economic and geopolitical uncertainty as a key concern entering Q4.

  • Tariffs are expected to impact costs, with an annualized tariff impact of $18 million, an incremental $12 million over 2024, but the company believes it can mitigate much of this through pricing actions and supply chain flexibility.

  • Service margin pressure continues due to project mix, though actions are being taken to improve margins, such as investing in equipment and refining the cost base.

  • Restructuring actions were undertaken to rightsize cost structure, with $5.9 million in charges, mostly severance.

  • Management flagged uncertainty around the broader economic impact on revenue, noting, "What we cannot calculate at this juncture is any impact of economic uncertainty and potentially slower growth on the revenue line."

Final Takeaway

Enerpac Tool Group recorded record third quarter revenues since its relaunch, supported by strong performance in the Americas and Cortland Biomedical, while maintaining a cautious approach due to ongoing economic and geopolitical uncertainty. The company reiterated its full-year guidance but now expects results toward the lower end of the range, citing tariff headwinds and cost pressures. Management continues to emphasize disciplined cost management, the strategic value of the DTA acquisition, and an accelerated pace of innovation through new in-house capabilities, positioning the company for competitiveness and resilience despite market softness.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Las Vegas casinos see gaming win decline again amid soft tourism from Asia, Canada and Mexico
data.description:
Gaming win on the Las Vegas Strip fell for the fourth straight month, according to a new report from the Nevada Gaming Control Board.

Statewide gaming win was down 2.2% year-over-year in May to $1.29 billion. The Las Vegas Strip saw a decline in gaming win of 3.9% to $742.5 million, and downtown Las Vegas casinos saw a gaming win drop of 11.4% for the month. Reno casinos saw gaming win decline 2.8% during the month to $65.3 million.

Las Vegas Strip gaming win down 4.6% for the slot machine business on a win percentage of just 6.6%. Table/counter/card game gaming win was up 2.8% on a strong win percentage of 15.5%. Baccarat revenue was down 6.3% during the month, with international visitation a concern. However, Craps gaming win was up 21% from a year ago off a win percentage of almost 18%. Notably, gaming win at sportsbooks rose 30% year-over-year during the month off strong results for baseball and hockey games.

The downturn in Las Vegas gaming revenue comes alongside a broader decline in travel to the city. Data from Harry Reid International Airport indicated that passenger numbers for May fell for the fourth consecutive month. Wall Street analysts have pointed to very soft tourism trends from Canada and Mexico as a general headwind for Las Vegas visitation numbers. 

Sector watch: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR), MGM Resorts (NYSE:MGM), Boyd Gaming (BYD), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Full House Resorts (FLL), Bally's Corporation (BALY), Sportradar (SRAD), Genius Sports (GENI), and Red Rock Resorts (RRR).

data.title: B. Riley sells GlassRatner to TorQuest for $118M
data.description:
B. Riley Financial (NASDAQ:RILY) sold its advisory services business, GlassRatner Advisory & Capital Group and B. Riley Farber Advisory, to funds managed by TorQuest Partners for $117.8M, the company said on Friday.

Proceeds from the sale will help provide capital to support its growth initiatives and strengthen its balance sheet.

B. Riley (NASDAQ:RILY) stock rose 1.0% in Friday morning trading.

"BRF's primary focus moving forward is to return to its historic roots by investing in BRS (B. Riley Securities), our middle-market investment bank, and its complimentary business, B. Riley Wealth Management," B. Riley Financial (RILY) Chairman and Co-CEO Bryant Riley. "Maximizing shareholder value through ongoing reinvestment in our business remains a critical priority."

B. Riley (RILY) has been refocusing after one of its big investments, Franchise Group, filed for bankruptcy in November. Last August, RILY suspended its dividend and announce an expected writedown on losses from its investment in Franchise Group.

In the past year, the company sold a stake in its Great American Group, a stake in its securities business, and executed a deal for bebe Stores'  brand assets, and a portion of its wealth management unit to Stifel (SF). It was also able to trim debt though a private bond exchange.

data.title: Maersk resumes vessel calls at Israel's Haifa port
data.description:
Shipper A.P. Moller-Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) said Friday it has resumed vessel calls to the port of Haifa in Israel and will accept both import and export cargo following a de-escalation of tensions in the region.

Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) had temporarily suspended vessel calls and cargo acceptance at Haifa due to security concerns in the wake of air strikes between Israel and Iran.

"With the prospect of current cease-fire agreement bringing de-escalation to the conflict, we have decided to resume vessel calls to the Port of Haifa, and acceptance for both import and export cargo is now open," the company said.

Vessels scheduled to call at Haifa will resume their regular timetable from Monday.

data.title: Microsoft fades to black as it removes 'blue screen of death' from Windows
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) plans to replace its notorious blue screen of death from Windows and replace it with a black one as the tech giant retools its security features following last summer's CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) debacle.

Last July, a worldwide outage led to the blue screen of death on Windows systems, while users worldwide were unable to access Microsoft 365 services. The outage was caused by a glitch in an update of CrowdStrike's cybersecurity platform Falcon Sensor. The outage hit backend operations at multiple industry sectors, including airlines, banks, hospitals and broadcast media, across the world.

As a result, Microsoft has launched the Windows Resiliency Initiative, or WRI. 

"WRI is an initiative designed to make all digital environments touched by Microsoft products more secure and resilient," the company said in a blog post. "WRI prioritizes preventing, managing and recovering from security and reliability incidents, mitigating issues swiftly and providing seamless recovery across the Windows platform."

One of the changes includes a faster recovery when Windows experiences unexpected restarts. For more than 40 years, this was accompanied with a blue screen and an error message. The new unexpected restart screen will now be black.

"The updated UI (user interface) improves readability and aligns better with Windows 11 design principles, while preserving the technical information on the screen for when it is needed," Microsoft said.

It will be released for all editions of Windows 11, version 24H2 devices later this summer. It also includes a better remedy for multiple unexpected restarts.

"In the case of consecutive unexpected restarts, devices can get stuck in the Windows Recovery Environment, impacting productivity and often requiring IT teams to spend significant time troubleshooting and restoring affected devices," the company said. "This is where quick machine recovery can help. When a widespread outage affects devices from starting properly, Microsoft can broadly deploy targeted remediations to affected devices via Windows RE—automating fixes with QMR and quickly getting users to a productive state without requiring complex manual intervention from IT."

Windows is also improving its endpoint security platform and its virus detection capabilities through the Microsoft Virus Initiative, where it works with cybersecurity partners such as CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) and SentinelOne (S).

"Security product updates must be gradual, leverage deployment rings and leverage monitoring to minimize negative impacts," Microsoft said.

data.title: Uber is downgraded by Canaccord in a contrarian call
data.description:
Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) fell in early trading on Friday after Canaccord downgraded the mobility transportation stock to a Hold rating from Buy.

While Uber (NYSE:UBER) was noted to be strong on the ground operations and other tactical elements, analyst Michael Graham warned that the future is unclear as an alternative scenario is that the new world is dominated by a few AV behemoths that control the value chain and leave both Uber (NYSE:UBER) and Lyft (LYFT) reflecting on the golden days of the past. "It is truly unclear," he summarized about the path ahead for the San Francisco-based company.

Wall Street analysts have a consensus Buy rating on Uber (UBER), with 44 Buy-equivalents ratings on the books vs. 10 Hold-equivalent ratings and no Sell-equivalent ratings. Seeking Alpha analysts also have a consensus Buy rating on Uber (UBER).

Shares of Uber (UBER) were down 1.7% in Friday morning action to $91.60. The 52-week high for the stock is $94.38.
